Brand Strategy
    Verbal Identity
      Visual Identity
        Becoming Quotient · Brand guideline

        The visual identity.

        How BQ looks, in every context. The mark, the catchphrase, the colour, the typography, the image treatment — and the construction that holds it all to spec.

        01 · Logo

        Three marks. One signature.

        BQ has three logo variants. Each does a different job. They never appear together, but they share the same DNA — the quote marks bracketing what’s inside.

        02 · Catchphrase

        The phrase, as a mark.

        “What an opportunity” is BQ’s signature phrase — and a mark in its own right. It locks up inside the same quote-frame as the logo. Use it on hero surfaces, campaign artwork, and anywhere the brand wants to speak in its own voice.

        03 · Construction & clear space

        Built on X.

        Every mark is constructed on a unit we call X. The X-grid governs proportion, internal spacing, and the clear space that must surround every lockup. Keep 2X minimum on all sides.

        Logo construction
        Catchphrase construction
        Clear space2X minimum on every side
        Internal spacingX/4 between wordmark and bq mark
        Minimum size — digitalPrimary 120 px · Secondary 32 px
        Minimum size — printPrimary 32 mm · Secondary 10 mm

        Note Minimum-size values are working defaults; tune to medium and viewing distance.

        04 · Don'ts

        The mark holds. Don’t bend it.

        Six ways the logo loses its meaning. The fixes are obvious; the discipline is the point.

        05 · Colour

        Six primaries. One ink.

        A working palette of six. Paper Beige is the default ground, BQ Ink Blue is the anchor; the four colour primaries — Yellow, Blue, Ochre, Olive — bring warmth, light, and contrast across the system.

        Primary palette
        Extended palette

        Note HEX values are the source-of-truth. Match BQ Ink Blue to a physical Pantone swatch on first print and lock that value for all subsequent production.

        06 · Typography

        P22 Mackinac & Indivisible.

        A warm humanist serif for the things we want remembered. A precise geometric sans for the things we want read. They share the same weight class, so they read as one voice.

        07 · Image treatment

        Photo, framed by the mark.

        BQ’s photography is real-people-led: natural light, candid expressions, no studio gloss. The brand mark frames the image — sometimes as a cutout, sometimes as a quote, always carrying the meaning of the moment.

        BQ Brand Guidelines Branding Partner: Everything Design
